title: Blue Wing
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the individual [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]|[[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], the fighter unit with which this pilot fought during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]}} {{Nickname}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name="Blue Wing" |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender= |pronouns=He/him''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]][[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] **[[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Blue Wing, take your group and draw the TIE fighters away—''"
"''I'll do what I can.|Lando Calrissian and Blue Wing|}} '''Blue Wing''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] as [[Blue Leader/Legends|Blue Leader]] [[Merrick Simms]]'s [[Executive officer/Legends|second-in-command]]. Blue Wing served with the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], coordinating several Rebel units and engaging in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]] against [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces above the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Following Simms's [[Death/Legends|death]], Blue Wing and [[Blue Squad]] participated in [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] attack on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]]. On Calrissian's orders, Blue Squad broke off their run to lure some of the Imperials' [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] away while Calrissian and the others destroyed the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. ==Biography== [[File:Merricksimms.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Merrick Simms, Blue Wing's commanding officer]] {{Quote|All fighters commence attack on the Death Star's main reactor. The deflector shield is down. Repeat. The deflector shield is down!''"
"''I see it. We're on our way. Red group! Gold group! Blue Squad! All fighters follow me!|Admiral Ackbar and General Calrissian, during the Battle of Endor|}} Though the term "Blue Wing" could refer to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], the designation was also the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of an individual [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] who served as [[Blue Leader/Legends|Blue Leader]] [[Merrick Simms]]'s [[Executive officer/Legends|second-in-command]]. The [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] pilot designated Blue Wing was tasked with directing multiple battle groups.{{CSWECite|I|81|Blue Wing}}''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Blue Leader"}} In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' the Rebel Alliance learned that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was constructing a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], and that the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] was protected by a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] projected from the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. A [[Endor strike team/Legends|commando team]] was dispatched to destroy the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] before the bulk of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], led by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], arrived in the [[Star system/Legends|system]] to destroy the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. Unbeknownst to the Alliance, they were falling into a trap orchestrated by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. When the Alliance Fleet arrived, they found the shield active and an Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] blocking their escape. The opposing armadas [[Battle of Endor/Legends|engaged]] one another in combat while the strike team struggled to accomplish their objective on Endor.{{Film|VI}} As the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] battle progressed, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Simms was killed following a Rebel attack on the Imperial [[Pride of Tarlandia/Legends|main communications ship]]. One of Blue Wing's battle groups, [[Blue Squad]], utilized [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing fighters]] to great effect in combating and defeating the Imperial Fleet during the battle.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' Eventually, the strike team succeeded in destroying the shield generator, leaving the Death Star vulnerable to Rebel assault. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], flying the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] as [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]], led several starfighter squadrons—including Blue Squad, under Blue Wing—in an attack on the Death Star. Scores of Rebel starfighters, followed closely by Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], skimmed low over the surface of the Death Star, looking for an entrance into its superstructure so they could find and destroy the station's [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. Attempting to alleviate his allies, Calrissian ordered Blue Wing to take Blue Squad and draw away the pursuing TIEs. Blue Wing acknowledged as Calrissian and the remaining fighters flew into the Death Star. The Rebels succeeded in their endeavor, destroying the battlestation's reactor core and, in turn, the Death Star itself, scoring a major victory for the Alliance. ==Behind the scenes== Blue Wing briefly appeared in the [[1983]] [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], receiving one line of dialogue. Blue Wing is unique to the novel, as his scene does not appear in the actual film. The pilot later received entries in all three editions of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'', and the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' For the ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' and the three ''A Guide to the Star Wars Universe'' entries on Blue Wing, "RJ" (short for ''Return of the Jedi'') is listed as the information source code, which would imply that Blue Wing appears in the film. It should be noted that the "RJN" code exists for entries from the film's novelization. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} *[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Squadron callsigns]]
